Commits: S1 cf40088a (graph→_store write path), S2 360a46f2 (optionality CI lane), S3 7fe02300 (doc-truth sweep), S4 cb196de9 (module-size gate), S5 974d40a4 (tombstone-boundary investigation+test), S6 fc5eb42e (5 duplication clusters DRYed), S7 f681ab87 (named budget + inbound field-map; 2 high-risk refactors deferred w/ rationale), S8 381f6bb9 (dead-code -207 LOC + hygiene). Verified: 2568 tests collect clean; interfaces parity (774), reconciler, graph, reducer suites green. NOTE: this epic introduced ZERO new ruff errors; a separate PRE-EXISTING ruff-gate breakage (29 errors from recent completion-verifier/sync commits) was filed as discovered work (deea-b923-2fcc-435e, discovered_from this epic) — not a regression from here.

comments.author: Joe Oakhart
comments.body: FORCE_CLOSE: close gate(s) bypassed by user approval — no completion/signature attestation was signed. Reason: "All 8 children deterministically verified closed+signed children (parent_id=e59d, status=closed, signature present). The verifier's real finding (CLAUDE.md warn-only drift, Success Criterion #3) is FIXED in commit 048f378f. Its other failure (Acceptance Criterion #1, 'cannot confirm 5 children exist') is a false-negative: it searches the codebase for child aliases instead of enumerating children by parent_id, missing children not referenced in code and exhausting its step budget on larger epics. Filed as bug a254-cee4-0f18-4fcb (discovered_from this epic). Force-closing because the gate cannot run correctly due to that verifier defect, not because work is incomplete.". Session: 048f378f.

compiled_state.description:
## Context

This epic tracks remediation of findings from the 2026-06-18 principal-engineer
maintenance audit of rebar (code debt, smells, architectural decay, separation
of concerns, doc accuracy, unit/dir size). The audit ran deterministic scans
(ruff/radon/vulture/ast-grep/jscpd/Serena) feeding seven single-perspective
subagents, then an **adversarial validation pass** (four subagents that read
every citation first-hand and tried to refute each finding). The re-run recipe
and tool list live in `docs/maintenance-audit-runbook.md`.

**Overall state: the codebase is healthy.** 0 mutable default args, 0 real bare
`except:`, only 2 high-confidence dead-code items, 2 TODO markers, no `__all__`
drift, no orphan modules, no unreachable code, the tree is clean for its own ruff
gate, and test investment tracks risk. The two load-bearing boundaries (the
engine `sys.path` seam and the LLM optionality/lazy-import contract) are intact
and runtime-verified. The debt is **localized and nameable**, captured in the
child stories below.

Severities below are the **validation-corrected** ones (several initial findings
were overstated and were downgraded after first-hand refutation; one was struck
entirely — see `docs/maintenance-audit-runbook.md` for the methodology lesson).

## Children (in recommended execution order)

1. **[HIGH] Route `graph/_links.py` link writes through the canonical `_store` write path** — the one confirmed structural violation: a second write path bypassing the mandated single locked core.
2. **[HIGH] Run the LLM-optionality degradation tests in a no-extras CI lane** — the optionality safety-net (10 always-skipped test instances) currently runs in zero CI lanes.
3. **[MED] Documentation-truth sweep** — README still documents a deleted bash engine; event-schema version + event-type drift; one actively-false source comment; archive completed planning docs.
4. **[MED-HIGH] Refresh the module-size offenders table + give the CI size report teeth** — table is 50% stale; CI check is a warn-only `echo`.
5. **[MED] Fix the unresolved-alias blocker edge that can mis-report a ticket as ready** — the one validated wrong-answer broad-except; harden the answer-affecting sites.
6. **[MED] DRY the validated duplication clusters (F9/F4/F5/F2-F3/F7)**.
7. **[MED] Reduce accidental complexity in the reconciler differ/applier**.
8. **[LOW] Dead-code removal + low-severity hygiene cleanups**.

## Success Criteria

- [ ] Every child story is closed (or explicitly deferred with a recorded reason).
- [ ] No regression in the existing ruff gate (`ruff check src tests` for `E,F,W,I,UP,B`), the scoped mypy gate, or the default test suite.
- [ ] `docs/architecture.md`, `README.md`, `docs/event-schema.md`, and CLAUDE.md agree with the code on the facts the audit flagged.
- [ ] The module-size CI report and the documented offenders table no longer disagree.
- [ ] The LLM-optionality degradation path is exercised in at least one CI lane.

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] All 8 child stories exist as children (`parent_id`) of this epic with detailed implementation plans and citations.
- [ ] The two HIGH stories (graph write-path unification; optionality CI wiring) are completed first.
- [ ] Each story's own acceptance criteria are met and verified before it is closed.
- [ ] This epic is closed only after all children are closed or deferred-with-reason.
</content>
compiled_state.title: Codebase maintenance remediation (2026-06-18 audit)
